The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Cockerellia nivea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
